name: Debuggers & Record-Replay
def: Tools to pause, inspect and step through running programs; record-replay captures runs for deterministic re-execution.
problem: Understand and fix bugs by observing program state and control flow, including hard-to-reproduce ones.
mechanism: Debuggers set breakpoints/watchpoints and inspect memory/stack; reverse and record-replay debuggers (rr, time-travel) capture a run and let you step backward deterministically.
limits: Print-debugging often faster for simple cases
failures: Heisenbugs that vanish under the debugger
myths: "Real engineers do not use debuggers" — they are essential for complex state bugs
